sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the blocks are now near the white cross! The orange block is just above the white cross. Let me check the exact position and see if I need to move RIGHT to align with the white cross.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check block positions
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ['O', 'b']:
        print(f"{node['color']} block: boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Check white cross area
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nWhite cross area:")
for i in range(29, 36):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][14:32]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The orange block is at rows 25-26, cols 19-23, and the blue block is at rows 27-29, cols 19-23. The white cross is at rows 31-33, cols ~17-21. The blocks are above and to the left of the white cross.
Looking at the ascii:
- Row 29: GGGGGbbbbbGGGGGGGG - blue block at cols 19-23 (5 cols)
- Row 31: GGGGGGGWGGGGGGGccc - white 'W' at col ~21
The white cross center appears to be at col 21 (the 'W' character). The blocks are at cols 19-23, which overlaps with col 21. So the blocks are horizontally aligned with the white cross!
But the blocks are at rows 25-29, and the white cross is at rows 31-33. The blocks are above the white cross. I need to move them DOWN to align with the white cross.
Wait, but looking at the image, the blocks are already very close to the white cross. The blue block ends at row 29, and the white cross starts at row 31. There's a gap of 1 row (row 30).
Let me move DOWN by 2 rows to align the blocks with the white cross.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move DOWN to align with white cross
action(['DOWN', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
